meaning_english: The act of renouncing or rejecting something. Synonyms: Renunciation, Self-denial, Refusal, Abandonment, Surrender.
Something out of its proper time. Synonyms: Misplacement, Solecism, Archaism, Incongruity, Post-dating.
Characterized by severe self-discipline and abstention. Synonyms: Austere, Hermit-like, Abstemious, Puritanical, Reclusive.
So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring. Synonyms: Trite, Hackneyed, Cliche, Commonplace, Humdrum.
Cease to resist an opponent or an unwelcome demand. Synonyms: Surrender, Yield, Succumb, Concede, Bow.
Friendly, lively, and enjoyable. Synonyms: Sociable, Genial, Affable, Amiable, Festive.
A political leader who seeks support by appealing to popular desires. Synonyms: Agitator, Firebrand, Rabble-rouser, Inciter.
Lacking a plan, purpose, or enthusiasm. Synonyms: Aimless, Rambling, Haphazard, Erratic, Disconnected.
Light, delicate, and translucent. Synonyms: Sheer, Gauzy, Transparent, Flimsy, Gossamer.
Insolent or impertinent behavior. Synonyms: Impudence, Audacity, Temerity, Gall, Cheek.
Outstandingly bad; shocking. Synonyms: Atrocious, Horrific, Appalling, Flagrant, Gross.
Mental calmness and composure, especially in a difficult situation. Synonyms: Composure, Calm, Serenity, Placidness, Poise.
Soon passing out of sight, memory, or existence. Synonyms: Vanishing, Fading, Fugitive, Short-lived, Perishable.
Pressing; demanding. Synonyms: Urgent, Crucial, Imperative, Acute, Taxing.
Silly and pointless. Synonyms: Foolish, Inane, Asinine, Vacuous, Ludicrous.
Deny or contradict a fact or statement. Synonyms: Deny, Dispute, Repudiate, Contravene, Oppose.
Unfortunate. Synonyms: Unlucky, Ill-fated, Wretched, Cursed, Miserable.
Just begun and so not fully formed or developed. Synonyms: Nascent, Embryonic, Rudimentary, Amorphous, Formless.
Insulting, abusive, or highly critical language. Synonyms: Abuse, Vituperation, Expletives, Denunciation, Vilification.
A dishonest or unscrupulous man. Synonyms: Scoundrel, Rogue, Villain, Rascal, Cheat.
Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion. Synonyms: Listless, Lethargic, Sluggish, Weak, Faint.
Self-pitying or tearfully sentimental. Synonyms: Emotional, Weepy, Mushy, Over-sentimental.
A person who is new to a subject or skill. Synonyms: Beginner, Novice, Tyro, Learner, Rookie.
Involving a great deal of effort, trouble, or difficulty. Synonyms: Burdensome, Heavy, Taxing, Arduous, Strenuous.
Translucently clear. Synonyms: Transparent, Crystal clear, Coherent, Lucid, Intelligible.
Complaining in a rather petulant or whining manner. Synonyms: Whiny, Grumbling, Fretful, Captious, Irritable.
Little known; abstruse. Synonyms: Obscure, Complex, Esoteric, Profound, Deep.
Health-giving; healthy. Synonyms: Wholesome, Beneficial, Salutary, Nutritious.
Very weak or slight. Synonyms: Fragile, Flimsy, Shaky, Insubstantial, Thin.
Find fault with someone; scold. Synonyms: Reprimand, Rebuke, Admonish, Castigate, Scold.
A change of circumstances or fortune. Synonyms: Alteration, Mutation, Shift, Fluctuation, Ups and downs.
Shriveled or wrinkled with age. Synonyms: Wrinkled, Gnarled, Dried up, Shrunken.
A feeling of repulsion; disgusted loathing. Synonyms: Hatred, Loathing, Detestation, Enmity.
Charm or enchant someone, sometimes in a deceptive way. Synonyms: Charm, Entice, Bewitch, Deceive.
Wary and unwilling to take risks. Synonyms: Cautious, Wary, Prudent, Guarded, Vigilant.
Inactive or inoperative. Synonyms: Latent, Passive, Quiescent, Idle.
Cause someone to feel drained of energy. Synonyms: Exhaust, Fatigue, Weaken, Devitalize.
Very attentive to detail and accuracy. Synonyms: Scrupulous, Meticulous, Fussy, Punctilious.
Fond of company; sociable. Synonyms: Sociable, Companionable, Convivial.
Overly theatrical or melodramatic in character or style. Synonyms: Dramatic, Melodramatic, Affected, Theatrical.
Impossible to stop or prevent. Synonyms: Relentless, Unstoppable, Persistent, Inevitable.
Cheerful and lighthearted. Synonyms: Cheerful, Merry, Glad, Jolly.
Very vivid in color, especially so as to create an unpleasantly harsh or unnatural effect. Synonyms: Sensational, Gory, Shocking, Vivid.
Exaggerate or feign illness in order to escape duty or work. Synonyms: Shirk, Idle, Dodge.
Of no value or importance. Synonyms: Worthless, Useless, Vain, Futile.
Remove a need or difficulty. Synonyms: Preclude, Prevent, Eliminate, Avoid.
Deceitfulness; untrustworthiness. Synonyms: Treachery, Duplicity, Betrayal, Infidelity.
Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical. Synonyms: Idealistic, Romantic, Visionary, Impractical.
Bitterness or resentfulness, especially when long-standing. Synonyms: Bitterness, Spite, Hate, Resentment.
A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important. Synonyms: Flatterer, Toady, Lackey.
